Banach Spaces with Property (w)
Denny H. Leung

Abstract

A Banach space E𝐸E is said to have Property (w) if every (bounded linear) operator from E𝐸E into E′superscript𝐸′E^{\prime} is weakly compact. We give some interesting examples of James type Banach spaces with Property (w). We also consider the passing of Property (w) from E𝐸E to C​(K,E)𝐶𝐾𝐸C(K,E).

1 Introduction

A Banach space E𝐸E is said to have Property (w) if every operator from E𝐸E into E′superscript𝐸′E^{\prime} is weakly compact. This property was introduced by E. and P. Saab in [9]. They observe that for Banach lattices, Property (w) is equivalent to Property (V*), which in turn is equivalent to the Banach lattice having a weakly sequentially complete dual. Thus the following question was raised [9]:

Question: Does every Banach space with Property (w) have a weakly sequentially complete dual, or even Property (V*)?

In this paper, we give two examples, both of which answer the question in the negative. Both examples are James type spaces considered in [1]. They both possess properties stronger than Property (w). The first example has the property that every operator from the space into the dual is compact. In the second example, both the space and its dual have Property (w). In the last section, we consider if Property (w) passes from a Banach space E𝐸E to C​(K,E)𝐶𝐾𝐸C(K,E). This was also dealt with in [9].
We use standard Banach space terminology as may be found in [6]. For a Banach space E𝐸E, E′superscript𝐸′E^{\prime} denotes its dual, and UEsubscript𝑈𝐸U_{E} its closed unit ball. If F𝐹F is also a Banach space, then we let L​(E,F)𝐿𝐸𝐹L(E,F) (respectively K​(E,F)𝐾𝐸𝐹K(E,F)) denote the space of all bounded linear operators (respectively all compact operators) from E𝐸E into F𝐹F. The norm in ℓpsuperscriptℓ𝑝\ell^{p} is denoted by ∥⋅∥p\|\cdot\|_{p}. Let us also establish some terminology about sequences. If (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) is a sequence in a Banach space, we use [ei]delimited-[]subscript𝑒𝑖[e_{i}] to denote the closed linear span of (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}). The sequence is semi-normalized if 0<inf‖ei‖≤sup‖ei‖<∞0infimumnormsubscript𝑒𝑖supremumnormsubscript𝑒𝑖0<\inf\|e_{i}\|\leq\sup\|e_{i}\|<\infty. If (fi)subscript𝑓𝑖(f_{i}) is another sequence in a possibly different Banach space, we say that (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) dominates (fi)subscript𝑓𝑖(f_{i}) if there is a constant C𝐶C such that ‖∑ai​fi‖≤C​‖∑ai​ei‖normsubscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑓𝑖𝐶normsubscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑒𝑖\|\sum a_{i}f_{i}\|\leq C\|\sum a_{i}e_{i}\| for all finitely non-zero real sequences (ai)subscript𝑎𝑖(a_{i}). Two sequences are equivalent if each dominates the other. Finally, we use the symbol ⪯precedes-or-equals\preceq (respectively ⪰succeeds-or-equals\succeq) to indicate ≤\leq (respectively ≥\geq) up to a fixed constant. The symbol ≈\approx stands for “⪯precedes-or-equals\preceq and ⪰succeeds-or-equals\succeq”.

2 James type constructions

We first recall the construction of James type spaces as in [1]. Let (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) be a normalized basis of a Banach space E𝐸E. For (ai)∈c00subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑐00(a_{i})\in c_{00}, the space of all finitely non-zero real sequences, let

|||∑ai​ui|||=sup{∥∑i=1k(∑j=p​(i)q​(i)aj)​ep​(i)∥:k∈ N I ,1≤p​(1)≤q​(1)<…<p​(k)≤q​(k)}.subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑢𝑖supremumconditional-setdelimited-∥∥subscriptsuperscript𝑘𝑖1subscriptsuperscript𝑞𝑖𝑗𝑝𝑖subscript𝑎𝑗subscript𝑒𝑝𝑖formulae-sequence𝑘 N I 1𝑝1𝑞1…𝑝𝑘𝑞𝑘\biggl{|}\biggl{|}\biggl{|}\sum a_{i}u_{i}\biggr{|}\biggr{|}\biggr{|}=\sup\biggl{\{}\biggl{\|}\sum^{k}_{i=1}\biggl{(}\sum^{q(i)}_{j=p(i)}a_{j}\biggr{)}e_{p(i)}\biggr{\|}:k\in\mbox{\hskip 1.00006ptN \hskip-12.50002ptI \hskip 1.00006pt}\,,1\leq p(1)\leq q(1)<\ldots<p(k)\leq q(k)\biggr{\}}.

The completion of the linear span of the sequence (ui)subscript𝑢𝑖(u_{i}) is denoted by J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}). Since we will only consider unconditional (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}), we use the equivalent norm

∥∑ai​ui∥=sup{∥∑i=1k(∑j=p​(i)p​(i+1)−1aj)​ep​(i)∥:k∈ N I ,1=p​(1)<p​(2)<…<p​(k+1)}.delimited-∥∥subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑢𝑖supremumconditional-setdelimited-∥∥subscriptsuperscript𝑘𝑖1subscriptsuperscript𝑝𝑖11𝑗𝑝𝑖subscript𝑎𝑗subscript𝑒𝑝𝑖formulae-sequence𝑘 N I 1𝑝1𝑝2…𝑝𝑘1\biggl{\|}\sum a_{i}u_{i}\biggr{\|}=\sup\biggl{\{}\biggl{\|}\sum^{k}_{i=1}\biggl{(}\sum^{p(i+1)-1}_{j=p(i)}a_{j}\biggr{)}e_{p(i)}\biggr{\|}:k\in\mbox{\hskip 1.00006ptN \hskip-12.50002ptI \hskip 1.00006pt}\,,1=p(1)<p(2)<\ldots<p(k+1)\biggr{\}}.

As in [1], ∑i=1k(∑j=p​(i)p​(i+1)−1aj)​ep​(i)subscriptsuperscript𝑘𝑖1subscriptsuperscript𝑝𝑖11𝑗𝑝𝑖subscript𝑎𝑗subscript𝑒𝑝𝑖\sum^{k}_{i=1}(\sum^{p(i+1)-1}_{j=p(i)}a_{j})e_{p(i)} is called a representative of ∑ai​uisubscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑢𝑖\sum a_{i}u_{i} in E𝐸E. The biorthogonal sequences of (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) and (ui)subscript𝑢𝑖(u_{i}) are denoted by (ei′)subscriptsuperscript𝑒′𝑖(e^{\prime}_{i}) and (ui′)subscriptsuperscript𝑢′𝑖(u^{\prime}_{i}) respectively. The basis projections with respect to the basis (ui)subscript𝑢𝑖(u_{i}) are denoted by (Pn)n=0∞subscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝑃𝑛𝑛0(P_{n})^{\infty}_{n=0} (P0=0)subscript𝑃00(P_{0}=0). The functional S𝑆S defined by S​(∑ai​ui)=∑ai𝑆subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑢𝑖subscript𝑎𝑖S(\sum a_{i}u_{i})=\sum a_{i} is bounded, hence S∈J​(ei)′𝑆𝐽superscriptsubscript𝑒𝑖′S\in J(e_{i})^{\prime}. The following lemma is useful for computing the norms of certain vectors in J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}). Recall that a basic sequence (xi)subscript𝑥𝑖(x_{i}) is right dominant [1] if it is unconditional and whenever 1≤m​(1)≤n​(1)<…<m​(i)≤n​(i)<…1𝑚1𝑛1…𝑚𝑖𝑛𝑖…1\leq m(1)\leq n(1)<\ldots<m(i)\leq n(i)<\ldots, we have

∥∑an​(i)​xm​(i)∥⪯∥∑an​(i)​xn​(i)∥.precedes-or-equalsdelimited-∥∥subscript𝑎𝑛𝑖subscript𝑥𝑚𝑖delimited-∥∥subscript𝑎𝑛𝑖subscript𝑥𝑛𝑖\biggl{\|}\sum a_{n(i)}x_{m(i)}\biggr{\|}\preceq\biggl{\|}\sum a_{n(i)}x_{n(i)}\biggr{\|}.
Lemma 1

Let (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) be a right dominant basis of a Banach space. There exists a constant C𝐶C such that whenever (vi)i=1lsubscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝑣𝑖𝑙𝑖1(v_{i})^{l}_{i=1} is a block basis of (ui)subscript𝑢𝑖(u_{i}) satisfying

vi=∑j=n​(i)n​(i+1)−1aj​uj,∑j=n​(i)n​(i+1)−1aj=0,1≤i≤l,formulae-sequencesubscript𝑣𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑛𝑖11𝑗𝑛𝑖subscript𝑎𝑗subscript𝑢𝑗formulae-sequencesubscriptsuperscript𝑛𝑖11𝑗𝑛𝑖subscript𝑎𝑗01𝑖𝑙v_{i}=\sum^{n(i+1)-1}_{j=n(i)}a_{j}u_{j}\,,\hskip 28.45274pt\sum^{n(i+1)-1}_{j=n(i)}a_{j}=0,\hskip 28.45274pt1\leq i\leq l, (1)

there is a block basis (wi)i=1lsubscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝑤𝑖𝑙𝑖1(w_{i})^{l}_{i=1} of (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) such that ‖wi‖≤‖vi‖,1≤i≤lformulae-sequencenormsubscript𝑤𝑖normsubscript𝑣𝑖1𝑖𝑙||w_{i}||\leq||v_{i}||,1\leq i\leq l, and ‖∑i=1lvi‖≤C​‖∑i=1lwi‖normsubscriptsuperscript𝑙𝑖1subscript𝑣𝑖𝐶normsubscriptsuperscript𝑙𝑖1subscript𝑤𝑖||\sum^{l}_{i=1}v_{i}||\leq C||\sum^{l}_{i=1}w_{i}||.

Proof: Let (vi)subscript𝑣𝑖(v_{i}) be as given. Choose p​(1)<p​(2)<…<p​(k+1)𝑝1𝑝2…𝑝𝑘1p(1)<p(2)<\ldots<p(k+1) such that

∥∑i=1lvi∥⪯∥∑i=1k(∑j=p​(i)p​(i+1)−1aj)​ep​(i)∥.precedes-or-equalsdelimited-∥∥subscriptsuperscript𝑙𝑖1subscript𝑣𝑖delimited-∥∥subscriptsuperscript𝑘𝑖1subscriptsuperscript𝑝𝑖11𝑗𝑝𝑖subscript𝑎𝑗subscript𝑒𝑝𝑖\biggl{\|}\sum^{l}_{i=1}v_{i}\biggr{\|}\preceq\biggl{\|}\sum^{k}_{i=1}\biggl{(}\sum^{p(i+1)-1}_{j=p(i)}a_{j}\biggr{)}e_{p(i)}\biggr{\|}.

Let (q​(i))𝑞𝑖(q(i)) be a finite strictly increasing sequence such that

{q​(i)}={p​(i):1≤i≤k+1}∪{n​(i):1≤i≤l+1}.𝑞𝑖conditional-set𝑝𝑖1𝑖𝑘1conditional-set𝑛𝑖1𝑖𝑙1\{q(i)\}=\{p(i):1\leq i\leq k+1\}\cup\{n(i):1\leq i\leq l+1\}.

Let A={i:A=\{i: there exists some m𝑚m with p(i)<n(m)<p(i+1)}p(i)<n(m)<p(i+1)\}. For all i∈A𝑖𝐴i\in A, let mi=min⁡{m:n​(m)>p​(i)}subscript𝑚𝑖:𝑚𝑛𝑚𝑝𝑖m_{i}=\min\{m:n(m)>p(i)\} and ri=max⁡{m:n​(m)<p​(i+1)}subscript𝑟𝑖:𝑚𝑛𝑚𝑝𝑖1r_{i}=\max\{m:n(m)<p(i+1)\}. By (1), for i∈A𝑖𝐴i\in A,

∑j=p​(i)p​(i+1)−1aj=∑j=p​(i)n​(mi)−1aj+∑j=n​(ri)p​(i+1)−1aj≡bi+ci.subscriptsuperscript𝑝𝑖11𝑗𝑝𝑖subscript𝑎𝑗subscriptsuperscript𝑛subscript𝑚𝑖1𝑗𝑝𝑖subscript𝑎𝑗subscriptsuperscript𝑝𝑖11𝑗𝑛subscript𝑟𝑖subscript𝑎𝑗subscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝑐𝑖\sum^{p(i+1)-1}_{j=p(i)}a_{j}=\sum^{n(m_{i})-1}_{j=p(i)}a_{j}+\sum^{p(i+1)-1}_{j=n(r_{i})}a_{j}\equiv b_{i}+c_{i}.

Therefore,

∥∑i∈A(∑j=p​(i)p​(i+1)−1aj)​ep​(i)∥delimited-∥∥subscript𝑖𝐴subscriptsuperscript𝑝𝑖11𝑗𝑝𝑖subscript𝑎𝑗subscript𝑒𝑝𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{\|}\sum_{i\in A}(\sum^{p(i+1)-1}_{j=p(i)}a_{j})e_{p(i)}\biggr{\|} ≤\displaystyle\leq ∥∑i∈Abi​ep​(i)∥+∥∑i∈Aci​ep​(i)∥delimited-∥∥subscript𝑖𝐴subscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝑒𝑝𝑖delimited-∥∥subscript𝑖𝐴subscript𝑐𝑖subscript𝑒𝑝𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{\|}\sum_{i\in A}b_{i}e_{p(i)}\biggr{\|}+\biggl{\|}\sum_{i\in A}c_{i}e_{p(i)}\biggr{\|}
⪯precedes-or-equals\displaystyle\preceq ∥∑i∈Abi​ep​(i)∥+∥∑i∈Aci​en​(ri)∥delimited-∥∥subscript𝑖𝐴subscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝑒𝑝𝑖delimited-∥∥subscript𝑖𝐴subscript𝑐𝑖subscript𝑒𝑛subscript𝑟𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{\|}\sum_{i\in A}b_{i}e_{p(i)}\biggr{\|}+\biggl{\|}\sum_{i\in A}c_{i}e_{n(r_{i})}\biggr{\|}
since (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) is right dominant
⪯precedes-or-equals\displaystyle\preceq ∥∑(∑j=q​(i)q​(i+1)−1aj)​eq​(i)∥.delimited-∥∥subscriptsuperscript𝑞𝑖11𝑗𝑞𝑖subscript𝑎𝑗subscript𝑒𝑞𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{\|}\sum\biggl{(}\sum^{q(i+1)-1}_{j=q(i)}a_{j}\biggr{)}e_{q(i)}\biggr{\|}.

Also, it is clear that

∥∑i∉A(∑j=p​(i)p​(i+1)−1aj)​ep​(i)∥⪯∥∑(∑j=q​(i)q​(i+1)−1aj)​eq​(i)∥.precedes-or-equalsdelimited-∥∥subscript𝑖𝐴subscriptsuperscript𝑝𝑖11𝑗𝑝𝑖subscript𝑎𝑗subscript𝑒𝑝𝑖delimited-∥∥subscriptsuperscript𝑞𝑖11𝑗𝑞𝑖subscript𝑎𝑗subscript𝑒𝑞𝑖\biggl{\|}\sum_{i\notin A}\biggl{(}\sum^{p(i+1)-1}_{j=p(i)}a_{j}\biggr{)}e_{p(i)}\biggr{\|}\preceq\biggl{\|}\sum\biggl{(}\sum^{q(i+1)-1}_{j=q(i)}a_{j}\biggr{)}e_{q(i)}\biggr{\|}.

Hence

∥∑i=1lvi∥⪯∥∑(∑j=q​(i)q​(i+1)−1aj)​eq​(i)∥.precedes-or-equalsdelimited-∥∥subscriptsuperscript𝑙𝑖1subscript𝑣𝑖delimited-∥∥subscriptsuperscript𝑞𝑖11𝑗𝑞𝑖subscript𝑎𝑗subscript𝑒𝑞𝑖\biggl{\|}\sum^{l}_{i=1}v_{i}\biggr{\|}\preceq\biggl{\|}\sum\biggl{(}\sum^{q(i+1)-1}_{j=q(i)}a_{j}\biggr{)}e_{q(i)}\biggr{\|}.

Now let Bm={i:n​(m)≤q​(i)<n​(m+1)}subscript𝐵𝑚conditional-set𝑖𝑛𝑚𝑞𝑖𝑛𝑚1B_{m}=\{i:n(m)\leq q(i)<n(m+1)\}. Then

∥∑i=1lvi∥delimited-∥∥subscriptsuperscript𝑙𝑖1subscript𝑣𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{\|}\sum^{l}_{i=1}v_{i}\biggr{\|} ⪯precedes-or-equals\displaystyle\preceq ∥∑m(∑i∈Bm(∑j=q​(i)q​(i+1)−1aj)​eq​(i))∥delimited-∥∥subscript𝑚subscript𝑖subscript𝐵𝑚subscriptsuperscript𝑞𝑖11𝑗𝑞𝑖subscript𝑎𝑗subscript𝑒𝑞𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{\|}\sum_{m}\biggl{(}\sum_{i\in B_{m}}(\sum^{q(i+1)-1}_{j=q(i)}a_{j})e_{q(i)}\biggr{)}\biggr{\|}
≡\displaystyle\equiv ∥∑wm∥.delimited-∥∥subscript𝑤𝑚\displaystyle\biggl{\|}\sum w_{m}\biggr{\|}.

Note that wmsubscript𝑤𝑚w_{m} is a representative of vmsubscript𝑣𝑚v_{m} in E𝐸E. Hence ‖wm‖≤‖vm‖normsubscript𝑤𝑚normsubscript𝑣𝑚||w_{m}||\leq||v_{m}||. Clearly, (wm)subscript𝑤𝑚(w_{m}) is a block basis of (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}). □□\Box

Lemma 2

Let (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) be a shrinking normalized unconditional basic sequence. Assume that L​(E,J​(ei)′)≠K​(E,J​(ei)′)𝐿𝐸𝐽superscriptsubscript𝑒𝑖′𝐾𝐸𝐽superscriptsubscript𝑒𝑖′L(E,J(e_{i})^{\prime})\neq K(E,J(e_{i})^{\prime}) for some subspace E𝐸E of J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}). Then there are semi-normalized block bases (xi)subscript𝑥𝑖(x_{i}) and (xi′)subscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖(x^{\prime}_{i}) of (ui)subscript𝑢𝑖(u_{i}) and (ui′)subscriptsuperscript𝑢′𝑖(u^{\prime}_{i}) respectively such that (xi)subscript𝑥𝑖(x_{i}) dominates (xi′)subscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖(x^{\prime}_{i}) and S​xi=0𝑆subscript𝑥𝑖0Sx_{i}=0 for all i𝑖i.

Proof: Since (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) is shrinking, by [1], Theorems 2.2 and 4.1, J​(ei)′𝐽superscriptsubscript𝑒𝑖′J(e_{i})^{\prime}=[{S}∪{ui′}i=1∞]absentdelimited-[]𝑆subscriptsuperscriptsubscriptsuperscript𝑢′𝑖𝑖1=[\{S\}\cup\{u^{\prime}_{i}\}^{\infty}_{i=1}]. Let T:E→J​(ei)′:𝑇→𝐸𝐽superscriptsubscript𝑒𝑖′T:E\rightarrow J(e_{i})^{\prime} be non-compact. Define the projection P:J​(ei)′→[ui′]:𝑃→𝐽superscriptsubscript𝑒𝑖′delimited-[]subscriptsuperscript𝑢′𝑖P:J(e_{i})^{\prime}\rightarrow[u^{\prime}_{i}] by P​(a​S+∑ai​ui′)=∑ai​ui′𝑃𝑎𝑆subscript𝑎𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑢′𝑖subscript𝑎𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑢′𝑖P(aS+\sum a_{i}u^{\prime}_{i})=\sum a_{i}u^{\prime}_{i}. Then (1−P)​T1𝑃𝑇(1-P)T has rank 111. Thus P​T𝑃𝑇PT is non-compact. Replacing T𝑇T by P​T𝑃𝑇PT, we may assume without loss of generality that range T⊆[ui′]𝑇delimited-[]subscriptsuperscript𝑢′𝑖T\subseteq[u^{\prime}_{i}]. Choose a bounded sequence (yi)subscript𝑦𝑖(y_{i}) in E𝐸E so that infi,j‖T​yi−T​yj‖>0subscriptinfimum𝑖𝑗norm𝑇subscript𝑦𝑖𝑇subscript𝑦𝑗0\inf_{i,j}\|Ty_{i}-Ty_{j}\|>0. By [1], Theorem 4.1, ℓ1superscriptℓ1\ell^{1} does not embed into J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}). Hence we may assume that (yi)subscript𝑦𝑖(y_{i}) is weakly Cauchy. Thus (y2​i−1−y2​i)subscript𝑦2𝑖1subscript𝑦2𝑖(y_{2i-1}-y_{2i}) is weakly null and semi-normalized; hence by Proposition 1.a.12 of [6], we may assume that it is equivalent to a semi-normalized block basis (xi)subscript𝑥𝑖(x_{i}) of (ui)subscript𝑢𝑖(u_{i}). Since (xi)subscript𝑥𝑖(x_{i}) is weakly null, S​xi→0→𝑆subscript𝑥𝑖0Sx_{i}\to 0. By using a subsequence, we may further assume that S​xi=0𝑆subscript𝑥𝑖0Sx_{i}=0 for all i𝑖i. Similarly, (T​(y2​i−1−y2​i))⊆[ui′]𝑇subscript𝑦2𝑖1subscript𝑦2𝑖delimited-[]subscriptsuperscript𝑢′𝑖(T(y_{2i-1}-y_{2i}))\subseteq[u^{\prime}_{i}] is semi-normalized and weakly Cauchy. Using the same argument, we may assume that it is equivalent to a semi-normalized block basis (xi′)subscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖(x^{\prime}_{i}) of (ui′)subscriptsuperscript𝑢′𝑖(u^{\prime}_{i}). Finally,

∥∑ai​xi′∥delimited-∥∥subscript𝑎𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{\|}\sum a_{i}x^{\prime}_{i}\biggr{\|} ≈\displaystyle\approx ∥∑ai​T​(y2​i−1−y2​i)∥delimited-∥∥subscript𝑎𝑖𝑇subscript𝑦2𝑖1subscript𝑦2𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{\|}\sum a_{i}T(y_{2i-1}-y_{2i})\biggr{\|}
≤\displaystyle\leq ‖T‖​∥∑ai​(y2​i−1−y2​i)∥norm𝑇delimited-∥∥subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑦2𝑖1subscript𝑦2𝑖\displaystyle\|T\|\,\biggl{\|}\sum a_{i}(y_{2i-1}-y_{2i})\biggr{\|}
≈\displaystyle\approx ∥∑ai​xi∥,delimited-∥∥subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑥𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{\|}\sum a_{i}x_{i}\biggr{\|},

as required. □□\Box

Theorem 3

Let (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) be a right dominant, normalized basic sequence which dominates all of its normalized block bases. Then the following are equivalent.
(a) For every subspace E𝐸E of J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}), L​(E,J​(ei)′)=K​(E,J​(ei)′)𝐿𝐸𝐽superscriptsubscript𝑒𝑖′𝐾𝐸𝐽superscriptsubscript𝑒𝑖′L(E,J(e_{i})^{\prime})=K(E,J(e_{i})^{\prime}),
(b) The sequence (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) does not dominate (ei′)subscriptsuperscript𝑒′𝑖(e^{\prime}_{i}).

Proof: Since (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) is unconditional, if it is not shrinking, it has a normalized block basis equivalent to the ℓ1superscriptℓ1\ell^{1} basis. Hence (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) dominates the ℓ1superscriptℓ1\ell^{1} basis. Thus (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) is equivalent to the ℓ1superscriptℓ1\ell^{1} basis. Both (a) and (b) fail in this case, so they are equivalent. Now assume that (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) is shrinking.

(a)⇒⇒\Rightarrow(b). Assume (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) dominates (ei′)subscriptsuperscript𝑒′𝑖(e^{\prime}_{i}). If ‖∑bi​ui‖≤1normsubscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝑢𝑖1||\sum b_{i}u_{i}||\leq 1, then ‖∑bi​ei‖≤1normsubscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝑒𝑖1||\sum b_{i}e_{i}||\leq 1. Hence

|⟨∑bi​ui,∑ai​u2​i′⟩|subscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝑢𝑖subscript𝑎𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑢′2𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{|}\biggl{\langle}\sum b_{i}u_{i},\sum a_{i}u^{\prime}_{2i}\biggr{\rangle}\biggr{|} =\displaystyle= |∑ai​b2​i|subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑏2𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{|}\sum a_{i}b_{2i}\biggr{|}
=\displaystyle= |⟨∑bi​ei,∑ai​e2​i′⟩|subscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝑒𝑖subscript𝑎𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑒′2𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{|}\biggl{\langle}\sum b_{i}e_{i}\,,\sum a_{i}e^{\prime}_{2i}\biggr{\rangle}\biggr{|}
≤\displaystyle\leq ∥∑ai​e2​i′∥.delimited-∥∥subscript𝑎𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑒′2𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{\|}\sum a_{i}e^{\prime}_{2i}\biggr{\|}.

Therefore, (u2​i′)subscriptsuperscript𝑢′2𝑖(u^{\prime}_{2i}) is dominated by (e2​i′)subscriptsuperscript𝑒′2𝑖(e^{\prime}_{2i}). By [1], Proposition 2.3, (u2​i−1−u2​i)subscript𝑢2𝑖1subscript𝑢2𝑖(u_{2i-1}-u_{2i}) is equivalent to (e2​i)subscript𝑒2𝑖(e_{2i}). Hence, if E=[u2​i−1−u2​i]𝐸delimited-[]subscript𝑢2𝑖1subscript𝑢2𝑖E=[u_{2i-1}-u_{2i}], then the map T:E→J​(ei)′:𝑇→𝐸𝐽superscriptsubscript𝑒𝑖′T:E\rightarrow J(e_{i})^{\prime} defined by T​(∑ai​(u2​i−1−u2​i))=∑ai​u2​i′𝑇subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑢2𝑖1subscript𝑢2𝑖subscript𝑎𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑢′2𝑖T(\sum a_{i}(u_{2i-1}-u_{2i}))=\sum a_{i}u^{\prime}_{2i} is bounded. Clearly, T𝑇T is not compact.

(b)⇒⇒\Rightarrow(a). Suppose L​(E,J​(ei)′)≠K​(E,J​(ei)′)𝐿𝐸𝐽superscriptsubscript𝑒𝑖′𝐾𝐸𝐽superscriptsubscript𝑒𝑖′L(E,J(e_{i})^{\prime})\neq K(E,J(e_{i})^{\prime}) for some subspace E𝐸E of J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}). By Lemma 2, there are semi-normalized block bases (xi)subscript𝑥𝑖(x_{i}) and (xi′)subscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖(x^{\prime}_{i}) of (ui)subscript𝑢𝑖(u_{i}) and (ui′)subscriptsuperscript𝑢′𝑖(u^{\prime}_{i}) respectively such that (xi)subscript𝑥𝑖(x_{i}) dominates (xi′)subscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖(x^{\prime}_{i}) and S​xi=0𝑆subscript𝑥𝑖0Sx_{i}=0. Let 1≤n1≤m1<n2≤m2<…1subscript𝑛1subscript𝑚1subscript𝑛2subscript𝑚2…1\leq n_{1}\leq m_{1}<n_{2}\leq m_{2}<\ldots be such that xi′∈[un′]n=nimisubscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖subscriptsuperscriptdelimited-[]subscriptsuperscript𝑢′𝑛subscript𝑚𝑖𝑛subscript𝑛𝑖x^{\prime}_{i}\in[u^{\prime}_{n}]^{m_{i}}_{n=n_{i}}. By using a subsequence, assume that

mi−ni+1≤ni+1−mi−1for alli≥1.formulae-sequencesubscript𝑚𝑖subscript𝑛𝑖1subscript𝑛𝑖1subscript𝑚𝑖1for all𝑖1m_{i}-n_{i}+1\leq n_{i+1}-m_{i}-1\hskip 10.00002pt\mbox{for all}\hskip 10.00002pti\geq 1. (2)

Let R::𝑅absentR:J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i})→→\toJ​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}) denote the right shift operator R​(∑ai​ui)=∑ai​ui+1𝑅subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑢𝑖subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑢𝑖1R(\sum a_{i}u_{i})=\sum a_{i}u_{i+1}. Since (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) dominates all of its normalized block bases, (Rk)superscript𝑅𝑘(R^{k}) is uniformly bounded. Choose a normalized block basis (yi)subscript𝑦𝑖(y_{i}) of (ui)subscript𝑢𝑖(u_{i}) such that yi∈[un]n=nimisubscript𝑦𝑖subscriptsuperscriptdelimited-[]subscript𝑢𝑛subscript𝑚𝑖𝑛subscript𝑛𝑖y_{i}\in[u_{n}]^{m_{i}}_{n=n_{i}} and ⟨yi,xi′⟩=‖xi′‖subscript𝑦𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖normsubscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖\langle y_{i},x^{\prime}_{i}\rangle=\|x^{\prime}_{i}\| for all i𝑖i. Now let zi=yi−Rmi+1−ni​yisubscript𝑧𝑖subscript𝑦𝑖superscript𝑅subscript𝑚𝑖1subscript𝑛𝑖subscript𝑦𝑖z_{i}=y_{i}-R^{m_{i}+1-n_{i}}y_{i} for all i≥1𝑖1i\geq 1. By (2), (zi)subscript𝑧𝑖(z_{i}) is a semi-normalized block basis of (ui)subscript𝑢𝑖(u_{i}). Also ⟨zi,xi′⟩=‖xi′‖subscript𝑧𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖normsubscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖\langle z_{i},x^{\prime}_{i}\rangle=\|x^{\prime}_{i}\| and S​zi=0𝑆subscript𝑧𝑖0Sz_{i}=0 for all i𝑖i. Fix (ai)∈c00subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑐00(a_{i})\in c_{00}. By Lemma 1, there is a block basis (wi)subscript𝑤𝑖(w_{i}) of (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) such that ‖wi‖≤‖ai​zi‖normsubscript𝑤𝑖normsubscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑧𝑖\|w_{i}\|\leq\|a_{i}z_{i}\| for all i𝑖i, and

∥∑ai​zi∥delimited-∥∥subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑧𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{\|}\sum a_{i}z_{i}\biggr{\|} ⪯precedes-or-equals\displaystyle\preceq ∥∑wi∥delimited-∥∥subscript𝑤𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{\|}\sum w_{i}\biggr{\|}
=\displaystyle= ∥∑‖wi‖​wi‖wi‖∥delimited-∥∥normsubscript𝑤𝑖subscript𝑤𝑖normsubscript𝑤𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{\|}\sum\|w_{i}\|\frac{w_{i}}{\|w_{i}\|}\biggr{\|}
⪯precedes-or-equals\displaystyle\preceq ∥∑‖wi‖​ei∥since (ei) dominates ​(wi‖wi‖)delimited-∥∥normsubscript𝑤𝑖subscript𝑒𝑖since (ei) dominates subscript𝑤𝑖normsubscript𝑤𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{\|}\sum\|w_{i}\|e_{i}\biggr{\|}\hskip 20.00003pt\mbox{since $(e_{i})$\ dominates }\biggl{(}\frac{w_{i}}{\|w_{i}\|}\biggr{)}
⪯precedes-or-equals\displaystyle\preceq ∥∑ai​ei∥.delimited-∥∥subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑒𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{\|}\sum a_{i}e_{i}\biggr{\|}.

Computing the norm of a vector of the form ∑bi​xi′subscript𝑏𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖\sum b_{i}x^{\prime}_{i} on some ∑ai​zisubscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑧𝑖\sum a_{i}z_{i}, we find that (xi′)subscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖(x^{\prime}_{i}) dominates (ei′)subscriptsuperscript𝑒′𝑖(e^{\prime}_{i}). Since S​xi=0𝑆subscript𝑥𝑖0Sx_{i}=0 for all i𝑖i. The computation above can also be applied to (xi)subscript𝑥𝑖(x_{i}). Hence (xi)subscript𝑥𝑖(x_{i}) is dominated by (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}). Since (xi)subscript𝑥𝑖(x_{i}) dominates (xi′)subscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖(x^{\prime}_{i}) by choice of the sequences, we see that (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) dominates (ei′)subscriptsuperscript𝑒′𝑖(e^{\prime}_{i}). □□\Box

Corollary 4

Let (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) be the unit vector basis of ℓp,2<p<∞superscriptℓ𝑝2𝑝\ell^{p},2<p<\infty. Then J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}) has Property (w) but not a weakly sequentially complete dual.

Proof: This follows immediately from Theorem 3 and the fact that J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}) is quasi-reflexive of order 1 by [1], Theorem 4.1. □□\Box

Remark. Theorem 3 fails without the assumption that (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) dominates all of its normalized block bases. In fact, if (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) is subsymmetric, then by [1], Propostion 2.3, (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) is equivalent to (u2​i−1−u2​i)subscript𝑢2𝑖1subscript𝑢2𝑖(u_{2i-1}-u_{2i}). Similarly, (ei′)subscriptsuperscript𝑒′𝑖(e^{\prime}_{i}) is equivalent to (u2​i′)subscriptsuperscript𝑢′2𝑖(u^{\prime}_{2i}) in this case. Now if we let (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) be the unit vector basis of the Lorentz space d​(w,2)𝑑𝑤2d(w,2) [6], then (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) is symmetric and shrinking, and does not dominate (ei′)subscriptsuperscript𝑒′𝑖(e^{\prime}_{i}). However, ℓ2superscriptℓ2\ell^{2} embeds into both d​(w,2)𝑑𝑤2d(w,2) and its dual, and hence into both J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}) and J​(ei)′𝐽superscriptsubscript𝑒𝑖′J(e_{i})^{\prime} by the observation made above. Hence condition (a) of Theorem 3 fails.

3 A non-reflexive space whose dual and itself have Property (w)

In this section, we give an example of a non-reflexive Banach space E𝐸E so that both E𝐸E and E′superscript𝐸′E^{\prime} have Property (w). In fact, neither E𝐸E nor any of its higher duals is weakly sequentially complete. The example will again be a J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}) space with a suitably chosen (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}).
If (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) is a normalized basis of a reflexive Banach space E𝐸E, then J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}) is quasi-reflexive of order 1 by [1], Theorem 4.1. Thus, if we define the functional L𝐿L on J​(ei)′𝐽superscriptsubscript𝑒𝑖′J(e_{i})^{\prime} by L​(a​S+∑ai​ui′)=a𝐿𝑎𝑆subscript𝑎𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑢′𝑖𝑎L(aS+\sum a_{i}u^{\prime}_{i})=a, then J​(ei)′′=[{L}∪{ui}i=1∞]𝐽superscriptsubscript𝑒𝑖′′delimited-[]𝐿subscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝑢𝑖𝑖1J(e_{i})^{\prime\prime}=[\{L\}\cup\{u_{i}\}^{\infty}_{i=1}]. Using this observation, the following Proposition can be obtained by straight forward perturbation arguments.

Proposition 5

Let (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) be a normalized unconditional basis of a reflexive Banach space E𝐸E.
(a) Let (yn)subscript𝑦𝑛(y_{n}) be a bounded sequence in J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}) with no weakly convergent subsequence. Then there exist a subsequence (yni)subscript𝑦subscript𝑛𝑖(y_{n_{i}}), an element y0subscript𝑦0y_{0} of J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}), a block basis (zi)subscript𝑧𝑖(z_{i}) of (un)subscript𝑢𝑛(u_{n}), and a≠0𝑎0a\neq 0 such that
(i) (yni−y0)≈(zi)subscript𝑦subscript𝑛𝑖subscript𝑦0subscript𝑧𝑖(y_{n_{i}}-y_{0})\approx(z_{i}), and
(ii) S​zi=a𝑆subscript𝑧𝑖𝑎Sz_{i}=a for all i𝑖i.
(b) Let (yn′)subscriptsuperscript𝑦′𝑛(y^{\prime}_{n}) be a bounded sequence in [un′]delimited-[]subscriptsuperscript𝑢′𝑛[u^{\prime}_{n}] with no weakly convergent subsequence. Then there are a subsequence (yni′)subscriptsuperscript𝑦′subscript𝑛𝑖(y^{\prime}_{n_{i}}), a vector y0′∈[un′]subscriptsuperscript𝑦′0delimited-[]subscriptsuperscript𝑢′𝑛y^{\prime}_{0}\in[u^{\prime}_{n}], 0=k0<k1<…,(zi′)⊆[un′]formulae-sequence0subscript𝑘0subscript𝑘1…subscriptsuperscript𝑧′𝑖delimited-[]subscriptsuperscript𝑢′𝑛0=k_{0}<k_{1}<\ldots,(z^{\prime}_{i})\subseteq[u^{\prime}_{n}], and b≠0𝑏0b\neq 0 such that
(i) For all i𝑖i, zi′∈[un′]n=ki−1+1kisubscriptsuperscript𝑧′𝑖subscriptsuperscriptdelimited-[]subscriptsuperscript𝑢′𝑛subscript𝑘𝑖𝑛subscript𝑘𝑖11z^{\prime}_{i}\in[u^{\prime}_{n}]^{k_{i}}_{n=k_{i-1}+1}, and
(ii) The sequence (yni′−y0′−b​Pki−1′​S−zi′)subscriptsuperscript𝑦′subscript𝑛𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑦′0𝑏subscriptsuperscript𝑃′subscript𝑘𝑖1𝑆subscriptsuperscript𝑧′𝑖(y^{\prime}_{n_{i}}-y^{\prime}_{0}-bP^{\prime}_{k_{i-1}}S-z^{\prime}_{i}) is norm null.

Proposition 6

Let (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) be a subsymmetric normalized basis of a reflexive Banach space E𝐸E, and let 1<p<∞1𝑝1<p<\infty. Assume that
(i) E𝐸E satifies an upper p𝑝p-estimate,
(ii) (ei′)subscriptsuperscript𝑒′𝑖(e^{\prime}_{i}) does not dominate (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}), and
(iii) The ℓpsuperscriptℓ𝑝\ell^{p} basis does not dominate (ei′)subscriptsuperscript𝑒′𝑖(e^{\prime}_{i}).
Then both J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}) and J​(ei)′𝐽superscriptsubscript𝑒𝑖′J(e_{i})^{\prime} have Property (w), but neither has a weakly sequentially complete dual.

Proof: Since J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}) is quasi-reflexive of order 1, neither J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}) nor J​(ei)′𝐽superscriptsubscript𝑒𝑖′J(e_{i})^{\prime} has a weakly sequentially complete dual. If T::𝑇absentT:J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}) →J​(ei)′→absent𝐽superscriptsubscript𝑒𝑖′\to J(e_{i})^{\prime} is not weakly compact, then we may assume that range T⊆[ui′]𝑇delimited-[]subscriptsuperscript𝑢′𝑖T\subseteq[u^{\prime}_{i}] as in the proof of Lemma 2. Now there is a bounded sequence (yn)subscript𝑦𝑛(y_{n}) in J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}) such that (T​yn)𝑇subscript𝑦𝑛(Ty_{n}) has no weakly convergent subsequence and infi,j‖yi−yj‖>0subscriptinfimum𝑖𝑗normsubscript𝑦𝑖subscript𝑦𝑗0\inf_{i,j}\|y_{i}-y_{j}\|>0. Apply Proposition 5 to (yn)subscript𝑦𝑛(y_{n}) and (yn′)≡(T​yn)subscriptsuperscript𝑦′𝑛𝑇subscript𝑦𝑛(y^{\prime}_{n})\equiv(Ty_{n}) to yield the various objects identified there. For all i𝑖i, let xi=yn4​i−1−yn4​i−3subscript𝑥𝑖subscript𝑦subscript𝑛4𝑖1subscript𝑦subscript𝑛4𝑖3x_{i}=y_{n_{4i-1}}-y_{n_{4i-3}}. Then (xi)subscript𝑥𝑖(x_{i}) is semi-normalized and (xi)≈(z4​i−1−z4​i−3)subscript𝑥𝑖subscript𝑧4𝑖1subscript𝑧4𝑖3(x_{i})\approx(z_{4i-1}-z_{4i-3}) (where (zi)subscript𝑧𝑖(z_{i}) is as given by Proposition 5). Also ‖T​xi−xi′‖→0→norm𝑇subscript𝑥𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖0\|Tx_{i}-x^{\prime}_{i}\|\to 0, where

xi′=b​(Pk4​i−1′−Pk4​i−3′)​S+z4​i−1′−z4​i−3′.subscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖𝑏subscriptsuperscript𝑃′subscript𝑘4𝑖1subscriptsuperscript𝑃′subscript𝑘4𝑖3𝑆subscriptsuperscript𝑧′4𝑖1subscriptsuperscript𝑧′4𝑖3x^{\prime}_{i}=b(P^{\prime}_{k_{4i-1}}-P^{\prime}_{k_{4i-3}})S+z^{\prime}_{4i-1}-z^{\prime}_{4i-3}.

Note that (xi′)subscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖(x^{\prime}_{i}) is a semi-normalized block basis of (ui′)subscriptsuperscript𝑢′𝑖(u^{\prime}_{i}) such that xi′∈[un′]n=k4​i−4+1k4​i−1subscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖subscriptsuperscriptdelimited-[]subscriptsuperscript𝑢′𝑛subscript𝑘4𝑖1𝑛subscript𝑘4𝑖41x^{\prime}_{i}\in[u^{\prime}_{n}]^{k_{4i-1}}_{n=k_{4i-4}+1} and ⟨uj,xi′⟩=bsubscript𝑢𝑗subscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖𝑏\langle u_{j},x^{\prime}_{i}\rangle=b if k4​i−3<j≤k4​i−2subscript𝑘4𝑖3𝑗subscript𝑘4𝑖2k_{4i-3}<j\leq k_{4i-2}. Without loss of generality, assume (T​xi)≈(xi′)𝑇subscript𝑥𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖(Tx_{i})\approx(x^{\prime}_{i}). For all i𝑖i, let k2​i−1<ji≤k2​isubscript𝑘2𝑖1subscript𝑗𝑖subscript𝑘2𝑖k_{2i-1}<j_{i}\leq k_{2i}. By the subsymmetry of (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}), it is easy to see that ‖∑bi​(uj2​i−1−uj2​i)‖≈‖∑bi​ei‖normsubscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝑢subscript𝑗2𝑖1subscript𝑢subscript𝑗2𝑖normsubscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝑒𝑖\|\sum b_{i}(u_{j_{2i-1}}-u_{j_{2i}})\|\approx\|\sum b_{i}e_{i}\|. Computing the norm of a vector of the form ∑ai​xi′subscript𝑎𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖\sum a_{i}x^{\prime}_{i} on ∑bi​(uj2​i−1−uj2​i)subscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝑢subscript𝑗2𝑖1subscript𝑢subscript𝑗2𝑖\sum b_{i}(u_{j_{2i-1}}-u_{j_{2i}}), we see that (xi′)subscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖(x^{\prime}_{i}) dominates (ei′)subscriptsuperscript𝑒′𝑖(e^{\prime}_{i}). On the other hand, since S​(z4​i−1−z4​i−3)=0𝑆subscript𝑧4𝑖1subscript𝑧4𝑖30S(z_{4i-1}-z_{4i-3})=0 for all i𝑖i. Lemma 1 implies that (xi)≈(z4​i−1−z4​i−3)subscript𝑥𝑖subscript𝑧4𝑖1subscript𝑧4𝑖3(x_{i})\approx(z_{4i-1}-z_{4i-3}) is dominated by some semi-normalized block basis of (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}). But since E𝐸E satisfies an upper p𝑝p-estimate, (xi)subscript𝑥𝑖(x_{i}) is dominated by the ℓpsuperscriptℓ𝑝\ell^{p} basis. Thus

∥∑ai​ei′∥delimited-∥∥subscript𝑎𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑒′𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{\|}\sum a_{i}e^{\prime}_{i}\biggr{\|} ⪯precedes-or-equals\displaystyle\preceq ∥∑ai​xi′∥delimited-∥∥subscript𝑎𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑥′𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{\|}\sum a_{i}x^{\prime}_{i}\biggr{\|}
≈\displaystyle\approx ∥∑ai​T​xi∥delimited-∥∥subscript𝑎𝑖𝑇subscript𝑥𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{\|}\sum a_{i}Tx_{i}\biggr{\|}
⪯precedes-or-equals\displaystyle\preceq ∥∑ai​xi∥delimited-∥∥subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑥𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{\|}\sum a_{i}x_{i}\biggr{\|}
⪯precedes-or-equals\displaystyle\preceq (∑|ai|p)1/p,superscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝑎𝑖𝑝1𝑝\displaystyle\biggl{(}\sum|a_{i}|^{p}\biggr{)}^{1/p},

a contradiction. Hence J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}) has Property (w).

Since J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}) is quasi-reflexive, if J​(ei)′𝐽superscriptsubscript𝑒𝑖′J(e_{i})^{\prime} fails Property (w), there is an operator T:[un′]→:𝑇→delimited-[]subscriptsuperscript𝑢′𝑛absentT~{}:~{}[u^{\prime}_{n}]~{}\to J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}) which is not weakly compact. Choose a bounded sequence (yn′)subscriptsuperscript𝑦′𝑛(y^{\prime}_{n}) in [un′]delimited-[]subscriptsuperscript𝑢′𝑛[u^{\prime}_{n}] so that (yn)≡(T​yn′)subscript𝑦𝑛𝑇subscriptsuperscript𝑦′𝑛(y_{n})\equiv(Ty^{\prime}_{n}) has no weakly convergent subsequence. As before, apply Lemma 5. In the present situation, we may assume zi′=0subscriptsuperscript𝑧′𝑖0z^{\prime}_{i}=0 as (zi′)subscriptsuperscript𝑧′𝑖(z^{\prime}_{i}) is a weakly null sequence. Arguing as before, we find that the sequence (z2​i−z2​i−1)subscript𝑧2𝑖subscript𝑧2𝑖1(z_{2i}-z_{2i-1}) is dominated by ((Pk2​i′−Pk2​i−1′)​S)subscriptsuperscript𝑃′subscript𝑘2𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑃′subscript𝑘2𝑖1𝑆((P^{\prime}_{k_{2i}}-P^{\prime}_{k_{2i-1}})S). Since S​zi=a≠0𝑆subscript𝑧𝑖𝑎0Sz_{i}=a\neq 0 for all i𝑖i, and because (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) is subsymmetric, it follows that (z2​i−z2​i−1)subscript𝑧2𝑖subscript𝑧2𝑖1(z_{2i}-z_{2i-1}) dominates (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}). On the other hand, if x∈𝑥absentx\in J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}), ‖x‖≤1norm𝑥1\|x\|\leq 1, then ‖∑⟨(Pk2​i−Pk2​i−1)​x,S⟩​ei‖⪯1precedes-or-equalsnormsubscript𝑃subscript𝑘2𝑖subscript𝑃subscript𝑘2𝑖1𝑥𝑆subscript𝑒𝑖1\|\sum\langle(P_{k_{2i}}-P_{k_{2i-1}})x,S\rangle e_{i}\|\preceq 1. Therefore,

|⟨x,∑ai​(Pk2​i′−Pk2​i−1′)​S⟩|𝑥subscript𝑎𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑃′subscript𝑘2𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑃′subscript𝑘2𝑖1𝑆\displaystyle\biggl{|}\biggl{\langle}x,\sum a_{i}(P^{\prime}_{k_{2i}}-P^{\prime}_{k_{2i-1}})S\biggr{\rangle}\biggr{|} =\displaystyle= |∑ai​⟨(Pk2​i−Pk2​i−1)​x,S⟩|subscript𝑎𝑖subscript𝑃subscript𝑘2𝑖subscript𝑃subscript𝑘2𝑖1𝑥𝑆\displaystyle\biggl{|}\sum a_{i}\biggl{\langle}(P_{k_{2i}}-P_{k_{2i-1}})x,S\biggr{\rangle}\biggr{|}
⪯precedes-or-equals\displaystyle\preceq ∥∑ai​ei′∥.delimited-∥∥subscript𝑎𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑒′𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{\|}\sum a_{i}e^{\prime}_{i}\biggr{\|}.

Hence (ei′)subscriptsuperscript𝑒′𝑖(e^{\prime}_{i}) dominates ((Pk2​i′−Pk2​i−1′)​S)subscriptsuperscript𝑃′subscript𝑘2𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑃′subscript𝑘2𝑖1𝑆((P^{\prime}_{k_{2i}}-P^{\prime}_{k_{2i-1}})S). Consequently, (ei′)subscriptsuperscript𝑒′𝑖(e^{\prime}_{i}) dominates (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}). This contradicts assumption (ii). Hence J​(ei)′𝐽superscriptsubscript𝑒𝑖′J(e_{i})^{\prime} also has Property (w). □□\Box

We now construct a sequence (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) satisfying the conditions in Propostion 6. For a number p∈(1,∞)𝑝1p\in(1,\infty), let p′=p/(p−1)superscript𝑝′𝑝𝑝1p^{\prime}=p/(p-1). Now fix p𝑝p and r𝑟r such that 1<p<2<r<∞1𝑝2𝑟1<p<2<r<\infty and r′<psuperscript𝑟′𝑝r^{\prime}<p. Let (kn)⊆ N I subscript𝑘𝑛 N I (k_{n})\subseteq\mbox{\hskip 1.00006ptN \hskip-12.50002ptI \hskip 1.00006pt}\ be such that

1+2​∑i=1n−1ir/2​kir/2≤kn1−p/2/np/2and12subscriptsuperscript𝑛1𝑖1superscript𝑖𝑟2subscriptsuperscript𝑘𝑟2𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑘1𝑝2𝑛superscript𝑛𝑝2and1+2\sum^{n-1}_{i=1}i^{r/2}k^{r/2}_{i}\hskip 10.00002pt\leq\hskip 10.00002ptk^{1-p/2}_{n}\bigg{/}n^{p/2}\hskip 20.00003pt\mbox{and} (3)
(n+1n)r/2​(knkn+1)(1/p−1/p′)​r/2≤12.superscript𝑛1𝑛𝑟2superscriptsubscript𝑘𝑛subscript𝑘𝑛11𝑝1superscript𝑝′𝑟212\biggl{(}\frac{n+1}{n}\biggr{)}^{r/2}\biggl{(}\frac{k_{n}}{k_{n+1}}\biggr{)}^{(1/p-1/p^{\prime})r/2}\hskip 10.00002pt\leq\hskip 10.00002pt\frac{1}{2}\ . (4)

Finally, let αn=n​kn(1/p′−1/p)/2subscript𝛼𝑛𝑛superscriptsubscript𝑘𝑛1superscript𝑝′1𝑝2\alpha_{n}=\sqrt{n}k_{n}^{(1/p^{\prime}-1/p)/2} for all n≥1𝑛1n\geq 1.

Lemma 7

For all l,j∈𝑙𝑗absentl,j\in  N I    with

2​∑i=1l−1ir/2​kir/2≤j≤kl1−p/2/lp/2,2subscriptsuperscript𝑙1𝑖1superscript𝑖𝑟2subscriptsuperscript𝑘𝑟2𝑖𝑗subscriptsuperscript𝑘1𝑝2𝑙superscript𝑙𝑝22\sum^{l-1}_{i=1}i^{r/2}k^{r/2}_{i}\hskip 10.00002pt\leq\hskip 10.00002ptj\hskip 10.00002pt\leq\hskip 10.00002ptk^{1-p/2}_{l}\bigg{/}l^{p/2}, (5)

and for all (xi)i=1lsubscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝑥𝑖𝑙𝑖1(x_{i})^{l}_{i=1} with 0≤xi≤ki,1≤i<lformulae-sequence0subscript𝑥𝑖subscript𝑘𝑖1𝑖𝑙0\leq x_{i}\leq k_{i},1\leq i<l, 0≤xl≤j−∑i=1l−1xi0subscript𝑥𝑙𝑗subscriptsuperscript𝑙1𝑖1subscript𝑥𝑖0\leq x_{l}\leq j-\sum^{l-1}_{i=1}x_{i}, we have

0≤∑i=1lαir​xir/p≤j2+2.0subscriptsuperscript𝑙𝑖1subscriptsuperscript𝛼𝑟𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑥𝑟𝑝𝑖𝑗220\hskip 10.00002pt\leq\hskip 10.00002pt\sum^{l}_{i=1}\alpha^{r}_{i}x^{r/p}_{i}\hskip 10.00002pt\leq\hskip 10.00002pt\frac{j}{2}+2.

Proof: Note that j−∑i=1l−1ki≥0𝑗subscriptsuperscript𝑙1𝑖1subscript𝑘𝑖0j-\sum^{l-1}_{i=1}k_{i}\geq 0 by the choice of j𝑗j, since r>2𝑟2r>2. Clearly, there is no loss of generality in assuming that xl=j−∑i=1l−1xisubscript𝑥𝑙𝑗subscriptsuperscript𝑙1𝑖1subscript𝑥𝑖x_{l}=j-\sum^{l-1}_{i=1}x_{i}. Let f:∏i=1l−1[0,ki]→ R I :𝑓→subscriptsuperscriptproduct𝑙1𝑖10subscript𝑘𝑖 R I f:\prod^{l-1}_{i=1}[0,k_{i}]\to\mbox{\hskip 1.00006ptR \hskip-12.50002ptI \hskip 1.00006pt}\ be defined by

f​(x1,…,xl−1)=∑i=1lαir​xir/p.𝑓subscript𝑥1…subscript𝑥𝑙1subscriptsuperscript𝑙𝑖1subscriptsuperscript𝛼𝑟𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑥𝑟𝑝𝑖f(x_{1},\ldots,x_{l-1})=\sum^{l}_{i=1}\alpha^{r}_{i}x^{r/p}_{i}.

Clearly, f≥0𝑓0f\geq 0 on ∏i=1l−1[0,ki]subscriptsuperscriptproduct𝑙1𝑖10subscript𝑘𝑖\prod^{l-1}_{i=1}[0,k_{i}]. Suppose that f𝑓f attains its maximum at (j1,…,jl−1)∈∏i=1l−1[0,ki]subscript𝑗1…subscript𝑗𝑙1subscriptsuperscriptproduct𝑙1𝑖10subscript𝑘𝑖(j_{1},\ldots,j_{l-1})\in\prod^{l-1}_{i=1}[0,k_{i}]. Let A={1≤i<l:ji=0}𝐴conditional-set1𝑖𝑙subscript𝑗𝑖0A=\{1\leq i<l:j_{i}=0\} and B={1≤i<l:ji=ki}𝐵conditional-set1𝑖𝑙subscript𝑗𝑖subscript𝑘𝑖B=\{1\leq i<l:j_{i}=k_{i}\}. Then for i∉A∪B𝑖𝐴𝐵i\notin A\cup B,

∂f∂xi​(j1,…,jl−1)𝑓subscript𝑥𝑖subscript𝑗1…subscript𝑗𝑙1\displaystyle\frac{\partial f}{\partial x_{i}}(j_{1},\ldots,j_{l-1}) =\displaystyle= 00\displaystyle 0
⇒αir​jir/p⇒subscriptsuperscript𝛼𝑟𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑗𝑟𝑝𝑖\displaystyle\Rightarrow\hskip 50.00008pt\alpha^{r}_{i}j^{r/p}_{i} =\displaystyle= αlr​jlr/p−1​ji.subscriptsuperscript𝛼𝑟𝑙subscriptsuperscript𝑗𝑟𝑝1𝑙subscript𝑗𝑖\displaystyle\alpha^{r}_{l}j^{r/p-1}_{l}j_{i}.

Hence,

f​(j1,…,ji−1)=∑i∈Bαir​kir/p+∑1≤i<li∉A∪Bαlr​jlr/p−1​ji+αlr​jlr/p,𝑓subscript𝑗1…subscript𝑗𝑖1subscript𝑖𝐵subscriptsuperscript𝛼𝑟𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑘𝑟𝑝𝑖subscriptmissing-subexpression1𝑖𝑙missing-subexpression𝑖𝐴𝐵subscriptsuperscript𝛼𝑟𝑙subscriptsuperscript𝑗𝑟𝑝1𝑙subscript𝑗𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝛼𝑟𝑙subscriptsuperscript𝑗𝑟𝑝𝑙f(j_{1},\ldots,j_{i-1})=\sum_{i\in B}\alpha^{r}_{i}k^{r/p}_{i}+\sum_{\begin{array}[]{c}\\[-10.85pt] {\scriptstyle 1\leq i<l}\\ \\[-9.94583pt] {\scriptstyle i\notin A\cup B}\end{array}}\!\!\alpha^{r}_{l}j^{r/p-1}_{l}j_{i}+\alpha^{r}_{l}j^{r/p}_{l},

where jl=j−∑i=1l−1jisubscript𝑗𝑙𝑗subscriptsuperscript𝑙1𝑖1subscript𝑗𝑖j_{l}=j-\sum^{l-1}_{i=1}j_{i}. Now αir​kir/p=ir/2​kir/2subscriptsuperscript𝛼𝑟𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑘𝑟𝑝𝑖superscript𝑖𝑟2subscriptsuperscript𝑘𝑟2𝑖\alpha^{r}_{i}k^{r/p}_{i}=i^{r/2}k^{r/2}_{i} . Hence

f​(j1,…,jl−1)𝑓subscript𝑗1…subscript𝑗𝑙1\displaystyle f(j_{1},\ldots,j_{l-1}) =\displaystyle= ∑i∈Bir/2​kir/2+αlr​jlr/p​(1+∑1≤i<li∉A∪Bji/jl)subscript𝑖𝐵superscript𝑖𝑟2subscriptsuperscript𝑘𝑟2𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝛼𝑟𝑙subscriptsuperscript𝑗𝑟𝑝𝑙1subscriptmissing-subexpression1𝑖𝑙missing-subexpression𝑖𝐴𝐵subscript𝑗𝑖subscript𝑗𝑙\displaystyle\sum_{i\in B}i^{r/2}k^{r/2}_{i}+\alpha^{r}_{l}j^{r/p}_{l}\biggl{(}1+\sum_{\begin{array}[]{c}\\[-10.85pt] {\scriptstyle 1\leq i<l}\\ \\[-9.94583pt] {\scriptstyle i\notin A\cup B}\end{array}}\!\!j_{i}\bigg{/}j_{l}\biggr{)}
≤\displaystyle\leq j2+αlr​jlr/p​(1+∑i=1l−1ji/jl)𝑗2subscriptsuperscript𝛼𝑟𝑙subscriptsuperscript𝑗𝑟𝑝𝑙1subscriptsuperscript𝑙1𝑖1subscript𝑗𝑖subscript𝑗𝑙\displaystyle\frac{j}{2}+\alpha^{r}_{l}j^{r/p}_{l}\biggl{(}1+\sum^{l-1}_{i=1}j_{i}\bigg{/}j_{l}\biggr{)}
≤\displaystyle\leq j2+αlr​jlr/p​(1+∑i=1l−1ki/(j−∑i=1l−1ki))𝑗2subscriptsuperscript𝛼𝑟𝑙subscriptsuperscript𝑗𝑟𝑝𝑙1subscriptsuperscript𝑙1𝑖1subscript𝑘𝑖𝑗subscriptsuperscript𝑙1𝑖1subscript𝑘𝑖\displaystyle\frac{j}{2}+\alpha^{r}_{l}j^{r/p}_{l}\biggl{(}1+\sum^{l-1}_{i=1}k_{i}\bigg{/}\biggl{(}j-\sum^{l-1}_{i=1}k_{i}\biggr{)}\biggr{)}
≤\displaystyle\leq j2+2​αlr​jlr/pby choice of j𝑗22subscriptsuperscript𝛼𝑟𝑙subscriptsuperscript𝑗𝑟𝑝𝑙by choice of j\displaystyle\frac{j}{2}+2\alpha^{r}_{l}j^{r/p}_{l}\hskip 20.00003pt\mbox{by choice of $j$}
≤\displaystyle\leq j2+2​αlr​jr/p𝑗22subscriptsuperscript𝛼𝑟𝑙superscript𝑗𝑟𝑝\displaystyle\frac{j}{2}+2\alpha^{r}_{l}j^{r/p}
≤\displaystyle\leq j2+2​(αl​kl(1/p−1/2)/l1/2)rby choice of j𝑗22superscriptsubscript𝛼𝑙subscriptsuperscript𝑘1𝑝12𝑙superscript𝑙12𝑟by choice of j\displaystyle\frac{j}{2}+2\biggl{(}\alpha_{l}k^{(1/p-1/2)}_{l}\bigg{/}l^{1/2}\biggr{)}^{r}\hskip 20.00003pt\mbox{by choice of $j$}
=\displaystyle= j2+2.𝑗22\displaystyle\frac{j}{2}+2.

□□\Box

Let (ti)subscript𝑡𝑖(t_{i}) be the normalized basic sequence so that for all (aji)j=1ki​i=1∞∈c00subscriptsuperscriptsubscriptsuperscript𝑎𝑖𝑗subscript𝑘𝑖𝑗1subscriptsuperscript𝑖1subscript𝑐00(a^{i}_{j})^{k_{i}}_{j=1}\mbox{}^{\infty}_{i=1}\in c_{00},

∥∑i=1∞(∑j=1kiaji​tk1+…+ki−1+j)∥=‖(aji)‖r∨∥(αi​(∑j=1ki|aji|p)1/p)i=1∞∥r.(k0=0)formulae-sequencedelimited-∥∥subscriptsuperscript𝑖1subscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝑘𝑖𝑗1subscriptsuperscript𝑎𝑖𝑗subscript𝑡subscript𝑘1…subscript𝑘𝑖1𝑗subscriptnormsubscriptsuperscript𝑎𝑖𝑗𝑟subscriptdelimited-∥∥subscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝛼𝑖superscriptsubscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝑘𝑖𝑗1superscriptsubscriptsuperscript𝑎𝑖𝑗𝑝1𝑝𝑖1𝑟subscript𝑘00\biggl{\|}\sum^{\infty}_{i=1}\biggl{(}\sum^{k_{i}}_{j=1}a^{i}_{j}t_{k_{1}+\ldots+k_{i-1}+j}\biggr{)}\biggr{\|}=\|(a^{i}_{j})\|_{r}\vee\biggl{\|}\biggl{(}\alpha_{i}\biggl{(}\sum^{k_{i}}_{j=1}|a^{i}_{j}|^{p}\biggr{)}^{1/p}\biggr{)}^{\infty}_{i=1}\biggr{\|}_{r}.~{}~{}~{}(k_{0}=0)
Example 8

Let E𝐸E be the Banach space with a basis (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) so that

∥∑bi​ei∥=sup{∥∑bi​tσ​(i)∥:σ is a permutation of  N I  }delimited-∥∥subscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝑒𝑖supremumconditional-setdelimited-∥∥subscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝑡𝜎𝑖σ is a permutation of  N I  \biggl{\|}\sum b_{i}e_{i}\biggr{\|}=\sup\biggl{\{}\biggl{\|}\sum b_{i}t_{\sigma(i)}\biggr{\|}:\mbox{$\sigma$ is a permutation of\ ${\rm\mbox{\hskip 1.00006ptN \hskip-12.50002ptI \hskip 1.00006pt}\ }$}\biggr{\}}

Then E𝐸E satisfies the conditions of Proposition 6. Hence J​(ei)𝐽subscript𝑒𝑖J(e_{i}) and J​(ei)′𝐽superscriptsubscript𝑒𝑖′J(e_{i})^{\prime} have Property (w), but neither has a weakly sequentially complete dual.

Proof: It is clear that (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}) is a symmetric basis and E𝐸E satisfies an upper p𝑝p-estimate. To show that E𝐸E is reflexive, it suffices to show that c0subscript𝑐0c_{0} does not lattice embed into E𝐸E. This will follow if we show that c0subscript𝑐0c_{0} does not lattice embed into [tn]delimited-[]subscript𝑡𝑛[t_{n}]. Let (xi)subscript𝑥𝑖(x_{i}) be a disjoint normalized sequence in [tn]delimited-[]subscript𝑡𝑛[t_{n}]. If inf‖xi‖r=ϵ>0infimumsubscriptnormsubscript𝑥𝑖𝑟italic-ϵ0\inf\|x_{i}\|_{r}=\epsilon>0, then

∥∑i=1kxi∥≥∥∑i=1kxi∥r≥(∑i=1k‖xi‖rr)1/r≥ϵ​k1/rdelimited-∥∥subscriptsuperscript𝑘𝑖1subscript𝑥𝑖subscriptdelimited-∥∥subscriptsuperscript𝑘𝑖1subscript𝑥𝑖𝑟superscriptsubscriptsuperscript𝑘𝑖1subscriptsuperscriptnormsubscript𝑥𝑖𝑟𝑟1𝑟italic-ϵsuperscript𝑘1𝑟\biggl{\|}\sum^{k}_{i=1}x_{i}\biggr{\|}\geq\biggl{\|}\sum^{k}_{i=1}x_{i}\biggr{\|}_{r}\geq\biggl{(}\sum^{k}_{i=1}\|x_{i}\|^{r}_{r}\biggr{)}^{1/r}\geq\epsilon k^{1/r}

for all k𝑘k. Hence (xi)subscript𝑥𝑖(x_{i}) is not equivalent to the c0subscript𝑐0c_{0} basis. On the other hand, if inf‖xi‖r=0infimumsubscriptnormsubscript𝑥𝑖𝑟0\inf\|x_{i}\|_{r}=0, we may assume that ‖xi‖r→0→subscriptnormsubscript𝑥𝑖𝑟0\|x_{i}\|_{r}\to 0. For each i𝑖i, we write xi=∑jxi​(j)subscript𝑥𝑖subscript𝑗subscript𝑥𝑖𝑗x_{i}=\sum_{j}x_{i}(j), with xi​(j)∈[tn]n=k1+…+kj−1+1k1+…+kjsubscript𝑥𝑖𝑗subscriptsuperscriptdelimited-[]subscript𝑡𝑛subscript𝑘1…subscript𝑘𝑗𝑛subscript𝑘1…subscript𝑘𝑗11x_{i}(j)\in[t_{n}]^{k_{1}+\ldots+k_{j}}_{n=k_{1}+\ldots+k_{j-1}+1}. Since ‖xi‖r→0→subscriptnormsubscript𝑥𝑖𝑟0\|x_{i}\|_{r}\to 0, limixi​(j)=0subscript𝑖subscript𝑥𝑖𝑗0\lim_{i}x_{i}(j)=0 for all j𝑗j. Thus, by perturbation and dropping to a subsequence, we may assume that each xisubscript𝑥𝑖x_{i} has the form ∑j=ji−1+1jixi​(j)subscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝑗𝑖𝑗subscript𝑗𝑖11subscript𝑥𝑖𝑗\sum^{j_{i}}_{j=j_{i-1}+1}x_{i}(j), where (ji)subscript𝑗𝑖(j_{i}) is strictly increasing. But then since (xi)subscript𝑥𝑖(x_{i}) is normalized and these factors add according to the ℓrsuperscriptℓ𝑟\ell^{r} norm, we see that (xi)subscript𝑥𝑖(x_{i}) is not equivalent to the c0subscript𝑐0c_{0} basis. This shows that E𝐸E is reflexive.
For all l𝑙l, choose j𝑗j as in Equation (5). We estimate the norm of ∑i=1jeisubscriptsuperscript𝑗𝑖1subscript𝑒𝑖\sum^{j}_{i=1}e_{i}. It is easy to see that there exists (ji)i=1∞⊆ N I ∪{0}subscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝑗𝑖𝑖1 N I 0(j_{i})^{\infty}_{i=1}\subseteq\mbox{\hskip 1.00006ptN \hskip-12.50002ptI \hskip 1.00006pt}\ \cup\{0\} such that ∑ji=jsubscript𝑗𝑖𝑗\sum j_{i}=j and

∥∑i=1jei∥=j1/r∨∥(αi​ji1/p)i=1∞∥r.delimited-∥∥subscriptsuperscript𝑗𝑖1subscript𝑒𝑖superscript𝑗1𝑟subscriptdelimited-∥∥subscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝛼𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑗1𝑝𝑖𝑖1𝑟\biggl{\|}\sum^{j}_{i=1}e_{i}\biggr{\|}=j^{1/r}\vee\biggl{\|}(\alpha_{i}j^{1/p}_{i})^{\infty}_{i=1}\biggr{\|}_{r}.

Now

∑i=1lαir​jir/p≤j2+2subscriptsuperscript𝑙𝑖1subscriptsuperscript𝛼𝑟𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑗𝑟𝑝𝑖𝑗22\sum^{l}_{i=1}\alpha^{r}_{i}j^{r/p}_{i}\leq\frac{j}{2}+2

by Lemma 7. For i>l𝑖𝑙i>l,

αi​ji1/psubscript𝛼𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑗1𝑝𝑖\displaystyle\alpha_{i}j^{1/p}_{i} ≤\displaystyle\leq αi​j1/psubscript𝛼𝑖superscript𝑗1𝑝\displaystyle\alpha_{i}j^{1/p}
≤\displaystyle\leq αi​(kl1−p/2/lp/2)1/psubscript𝛼𝑖superscriptsubscriptsuperscript𝑘1𝑝2𝑙superscript𝑙𝑝21𝑝\displaystyle\alpha_{i}(k^{1-p/2}_{l}/l^{p/2})^{1/p}
=\displaystyle= il​(klki)(1/p−1/p′)/2𝑖𝑙superscriptsubscript𝑘𝑙subscript𝑘𝑖1𝑝1superscript𝑝′2\displaystyle\sqrt{\frac{i}{l}}\biggl{(}\frac{k_{l}}{k_{i}}\biggr{)}^{(1/p-1/p^{\prime})/2}
=\displaystyle= (∏m=il+1mm−1)1/2​(∏m=il+1km−1km)(1/p−1/p′)/2superscriptsubscriptsuperscriptproduct𝑙1𝑚𝑖𝑚𝑚112superscriptsubscriptsuperscriptproduct𝑙1𝑚𝑖subscript𝑘𝑚1subscript𝑘𝑚1𝑝1superscript𝑝′2\displaystyle\biggl{(}\prod^{l+1}_{m=i}\frac{m}{m-1}\biggr{)}^{1/2}\biggl{(}\prod^{l+1}_{m=i}\frac{k_{m-1}}{k_{m}}\biggr{)}^{(1/p-1/p^{\prime})/2}
≤\displaystyle\leq 2−(i−l)/r,superscript2𝑖𝑙𝑟\displaystyle 2^{-(i-l)/r},

by Equation (4). It follows easily that ‖(αi​ji1/p)i=1∞‖r⪯j1/rprecedes-or-equalssubscriptnormsubscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝛼𝑖subscriptsuperscript𝑗1𝑝𝑖𝑖1𝑟superscript𝑗1𝑟\|(\alpha_{i}j^{1/p}_{i})^{\infty}_{i=1}\|_{r}\preceq j^{1/r}. Hence ‖∑i=1jei‖⪯j1/rprecedes-or-equalsnormsubscriptsuperscript𝑗𝑖1subscript𝑒𝑖superscript𝑗1𝑟\|\sum^{j}_{i=1}e_{i}\|\preceq j^{1/r}. This implies that

∥∑i=1jei′∥⪰j1/r′.succeeds-or-equalsdelimited-∥∥subscriptsuperscript𝑗𝑖1subscriptsuperscript𝑒′𝑖superscript𝑗1superscript𝑟′\biggl{\|}\sum^{j}_{i=1}e^{\prime}_{i}\biggr{\|}\succeq j^{1/r^{\prime}}.

Since r′<psuperscript𝑟′𝑝r^{\prime}<p, the ℓpsuperscriptℓ𝑝\ell^{p} basis does not dominate (ei′)subscriptsuperscript𝑒′𝑖(e^{\prime}_{i}).
Finally, for all n𝑛n,

∥∑i=1knei∥≥αn​kn1/p=n​kn.delimited-∥∥subscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝑘𝑛𝑖1subscript𝑒𝑖subscript𝛼𝑛subscriptsuperscript𝑘1𝑝𝑛𝑛subscript𝑘𝑛\biggl{\|}\sum^{k_{n}}_{i=1}e_{i}\biggr{\|}\geq\alpha_{n}k^{1/p}_{n}=\sqrt{nk_{n}}.

Also,

∥∑i=1knbi​ei∥delimited-∥∥subscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝑘𝑛𝑖1subscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝑒𝑖\displaystyle\biggl{\|}\sum^{k_{n}}_{i=1}b_{i}e_{i}\biggr{\|} ≤\displaystyle\leq 11\displaystyle 1
⇒αn​(∑i=1kn|bi|p)1/p⇒subscript𝛼𝑛superscriptsubscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝑘𝑛𝑖1superscriptsubscript𝑏𝑖𝑝1𝑝\displaystyle\Rightarrow\hskip 33.00008pt\alpha_{n}\biggl{(}\sum^{k_{n}}_{i=1}|b_{i}|^{p}\biggr{)}^{1/p} ≤\displaystyle\leq 11\displaystyle 1
⇒⟨∑i=1knbi​ei,∑i=1knei′⟩⇒subscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝑘𝑛𝑖1subscript𝑏𝑖subscript𝑒𝑖subscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝑘𝑛𝑖1subscriptsuperscript𝑒′𝑖\displaystyle\Rightarrow\hskip 31.0001pt\biggl{\langle}\sum^{k_{n}}_{i=1}b_{i}e_{i},\sum^{k_{n}}_{i=1}e^{\prime}_{i}\biggr{\rangle} ≤\displaystyle\leq ‖(bi)‖p​kn1/p′subscriptnormsubscript𝑏𝑖𝑝subscriptsuperscript𝑘1superscript𝑝′𝑛\displaystyle\|(b_{i})\|_{p}\,k^{1/p^{\prime}}_{n}
≤\displaystyle\leq kn1/p′αnsubscriptsuperscript𝑘1superscript𝑝′𝑛subscript𝛼𝑛\displaystyle\frac{k^{1/p^{\prime}}_{n}}{\alpha_{n}}
=\displaystyle= knn.subscript𝑘𝑛𝑛\displaystyle\sqrt{\frac{k_{n}}{n}}.

Hence ‖∑i=1knei′‖≤(kn/n)1/2normsubscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝑘𝑛𝑖1subscriptsuperscript𝑒′𝑖superscriptsubscript𝑘𝑛𝑛12\|\sum^{k_{n}}_{i=1}e^{\prime}_{i}\|\leq(k_{n}/n)^{1/2}. Therefore, (ei′)subscriptsuperscript𝑒′𝑖(e^{\prime}_{i}) does not dominate (ei)subscript𝑒𝑖(e_{i}). □□\Box

4 Property (w) in C​(K,E)𝐶𝐾𝐸C(K,E)

In this section, we consider the question of whether the Property (w) passes from a Banach space E𝐸E to C​(K,E)𝐶𝐾𝐸C(K,E), the space of all continuous E𝐸E-valued functions on a compact Hausdorff space K𝐾K. Let E,F𝐸𝐹E,F be Banach spaces, and let K𝐾K be an arbitrary compact Hausdorff space whose collection of Borel subsets is denoted by ΣΣ\Sigma. The dual of C​(K,E)𝐶𝐾𝐸C(K,E) is isometric to the space M​(K,E′)𝑀𝐾superscript𝐸′M(K,E^{\prime}) of all regular E′superscript𝐸′E^{\prime}-valued measures of bounded variation on K𝐾K [4]. In case E= R I 𝐸 R I E=\mbox{\hskip 1.00006ptR \hskip-12.50002ptI \hskip 1.00006pt}  , we use the notation C​(K)𝐶𝐾C(K) and M​(K)𝑀𝐾M(K) respectively. For μ𝜇\mu in M​(K,E′)𝑀𝐾superscript𝐸′M(K,E^{\prime}), let |μ|∈M​(K)𝜇𝑀𝐾|\mu|\in M(K) denote its variation ([4], p. 2). Given T∈L​(C​(K,E),F)𝑇𝐿𝐶𝐾𝐸𝐹T\in L(C(K,E),F), it is well known that T𝑇T can be represented by a vector measure G:Σ→L​(E,F′′):𝐺→Σ𝐿𝐸superscript𝐹′′G:\Sigma\to L(E,F^{\prime\prime}) [4]. In fact, G𝐺G is given by

G​(A)​x=T′′​(χA⊗x)𝐺𝐴𝑥superscript𝑇′′tensor-productsubscript𝜒𝐴𝑥G(A)x=T^{\prime\prime}(\chi_{A}\otimes x)

for all A∈Σ𝐴ΣA\in\Sigma and x∈E𝑥𝐸x\in E, where χAsubscript𝜒𝐴\chi_{A} is the characteristic function of the set A𝐴A. For all y′∈F′superscript𝑦′superscript𝐹′y^{\prime}\in F^{\prime}, we define Gy′∈M​(K,E′)subscript𝐺superscript𝑦′𝑀𝐾superscript𝐸′G_{y^{\prime}}\in M(K,E^{\prime}) by

⟨x,Gy′​(A)⟩=⟨y′,G​(A)​x⟩𝑥subscript𝐺superscript𝑦′𝐴superscript𝑦′𝐺𝐴𝑥\langle x,G_{y^{\prime}}(A)\rangle=\langle y^{\prime},G(A)x\rangle

for all x∈E,A∈Σformulae-sequence𝑥𝐸𝐴Σx\in E,A\in\Sigma. Then the semivariation of G𝐺G is given by

∥G∥(A)=sup{|Gy′|(A):∥y′∥≤1}.\|G\|(A)=\sup\{|G_{y^{\prime}}|(A):\|y^{\prime}\|\leq 1\}.

The following result is well known [2].

Proposition 9

Let T:C​(K,E)→F:𝑇→𝐶𝐾𝐸𝐹T:C(K,E)\to F be weakly compact. Then its representing measure G𝐺G satisfies
(a) G𝐺G takes values in L​(E,F)𝐿𝐸𝐹L(E,F),
(b) For every A∈Σ𝐴ΣA\in\Sigma, G​(A)𝐺𝐴G(A) is weakly compact, and
(c) The semivariation of G𝐺G is continous at ∅\emptyset, i.e., limn‖G‖​(An)=0subscript𝑛norm𝐺subscript𝐴𝑛0\lim_{n}\|G\|(A_{n})=0 for every sequence (An)subscript𝐴𝑛(A_{n}) in ΣΣ\Sigma which decreases to ∅\emptyset.

Condition (c) is just the uniform countable additivity of the set {|Gy′|:‖y′‖≤1}:subscript𝐺superscript𝑦′normsuperscript𝑦′1\{|G_{y^{\prime}}|:\|y^{\prime}\|\leq 1\}. By Lemma VI.2.13 of [4], this is equivalent to the fact that

limnsup‖y′‖≤1|Gy′|​(An)=0subscript𝑛subscriptsupremumnormsuperscript𝑦′1subscript𝐺superscript𝑦′subscript𝐴𝑛0\lim_{n}\sup_{\|y^{\prime}\|\leq 1}|G_{y^{\prime}}|(A_{n})=0

whenever (An)subscript𝐴𝑛(A_{n}) is a pairwise disjoint sequence in ΣΣ\Sigma. For the sake of brevity, we introduce the following ad hoc terminology.

Definition. A pair (K,E)𝐾𝐸(K,E), where K𝐾K is a compact Hausdorff space and E𝐸E is a Banach space, is called simple if for every Banach space F𝐹F, every operator T:C​(K,E)→F:𝑇→𝐶𝐾𝐸𝐹T:C(K,E)\to F whose representing measure G𝐺G satifies conditions (a)–(c) of Proposition 9 is weakly compact.

Theorem 10

Suppose (K,E)𝐾𝐸(K,E) is simple. Then E𝐸E has Property (w)  if and only if C​(K,E)𝐶𝐾𝐸C(K,E) does.

Proof: One direction is trivial. Now assume that E𝐸E has Property (w). Then E′superscript𝐸′E^{\prime} cannot contain a copy of c0subscript𝑐0c_{0} [9]. We first prove the

Claim. Every (bounded linear) operator from C​(K,E)𝐶𝐾𝐸C(K,E) into E′superscript𝐸′E^{\prime} is weakly compact.

Let T:C​(K,E)→E′:𝑇→𝐶𝐾𝐸superscript𝐸′T:C(K,E)\to E^{\prime} be represented by the measure G𝐺G. For all x∈E𝑥𝐸x\in E, define Tx:C​(K)→E′:subscript𝑇𝑥→𝐶𝐾superscript𝐸′T_{x}:C(K)\to E^{\prime} by Tx​f=T​(f⊗x)subscript𝑇𝑥𝑓𝑇tensor-product𝑓𝑥T_{x}f=T(f\otimes x) for all f∈C​(K)𝑓𝐶𝐾f\in C(K). Since E′superscript𝐸′E^{\prime} does not contain a copy of c0subscript𝑐0c_{0}, Txsubscript𝑇𝑥T_{x} is weakly compact [7]. Hence G𝐺G takes values in L​(E,E′)𝐿𝐸superscript𝐸′L(E,E^{\prime}) [5]. Since E𝐸E has Property (w), G​(A)𝐺𝐴G(A) is weakly compact for all A∈Σ𝐴ΣA\in\Sigma. If ‖G‖norm𝐺\|G\| is not continuous at ∅\emptyset, then there are (xn′′)⊆UE′′subscriptsuperscript𝑥′′𝑛subscript𝑈superscript𝐸′′(x^{\prime\prime}_{n})\subseteq U_{E^{\prime\prime}}, a pairwise disjoint sequence (An)subscript𝐴𝑛(A_{n}) in ΣΣ\Sigma, and ϵ>0italic-ϵ0\epsilon>0 such that |Gxn′′​(An)|>ϵsubscript𝐺subscriptsuperscript𝑥′′𝑛subscript𝐴𝑛italic-ϵ|G_{x^{\prime\prime}_{n}}(A_{n})|>\epsilon for all n𝑛n. Choose ΣΣ\Sigma-measurable UEsubscript𝑈𝐸U_{E}-valued simple functions (fn)subscript𝑓𝑛(f_{n}) such that supp f⊆An𝑓subscript𝐴𝑛f\subseteq A_{n} and ∫fn​𝑑Gxn′′>ϵsubscript𝑓𝑛differential-dsubscript𝐺subscriptsuperscript𝑥′′𝑛italic-ϵ\int\!f_{n}dG_{x^{\prime\prime}_{n}}>\epsilon for all n𝑛n. Since UEsubscript𝑈𝐸U_{E} is σ​(E′′,E′)𝜎superscript𝐸′′superscript𝐸′\sigma(E^{\prime\prime},E^{\prime})-dense in UE′′subscript𝑈superscript𝐸′′U_{E^{\prime\prime}} and fnsubscript𝑓𝑛f_{n} is simple, there exist (xn)⊆UEsubscript𝑥𝑛subscript𝑈𝐸(x_{n})\subseteq U_{E} such that ∫fn​𝑑Gxn>ϵsubscript𝑓𝑛differential-dsubscript𝐺subscript𝑥𝑛italic-ϵ\int\!f_{n}dG_{x_{n}}>\epsilon. Note that for all x∈E𝑥𝐸x\in E,

∑n|∫fn​𝑑Gx|≤∑n|Gx|​(An)≤|Gx|​(K)=‖T′​y‖.subscript𝑛subscript𝑓𝑛differential-dsubscript𝐺𝑥subscript𝑛subscript𝐺𝑥subscript𝐴𝑛subscript𝐺𝑥𝐾normsuperscript𝑇′𝑦\sum_{n}\biggl{|}\int\!f_{n}dG_{x}\biggr{|}\leq\sum_{n}|G_{x}|(A_{n})\leq|G_{x}|(K)=\|T^{\prime}y\|.

Hence S:E→ℓ1:𝑆→𝐸superscriptℓ1S:E\to\ell^{1} defined by

S​x=(∫fn​𝑑Gn)n𝑆𝑥subscriptsubscript𝑓𝑛differential-dsubscript𝐺𝑛𝑛Sx=\biggl{(}\int\!f_{n}dG_{n}\biggr{)}_{n}

is bounded. Since (S​xn)​(n)>ϵ𝑆subscript𝑥𝑛𝑛italic-ϵ(Sx_{n})(n)>\epsilon for all n𝑛n, (S​xn)𝑆subscript𝑥𝑛(Sx_{n}) is not weakly compact in ℓ1superscriptℓ1\ell^{1}. Using Proposition 2.a.2 of [6], we find a subsequence (xnm)subscript𝑥subscript𝑛𝑚(x_{n_{m}}) such that (S​xnm)𝑆subscript𝑥subscript𝑛𝑚(Sx_{n_{m}}) is equivalent to the ℓ1superscriptℓ1\ell^{1} basis, and [S​xnm]delimited-[]𝑆subscript𝑥subscript𝑛𝑚[Sx_{n_{m}}] is complemented in ℓ1superscriptℓ1\ell^{1}. ¿From this it follows readily that E𝐸E contains a complemented copy of ℓ1superscriptℓ1\ell^{1} as well. This contradicts the fact that E𝐸E has Property (w). Since G𝐺G satisfies conditions (a)–(c) of Proposition 9, and (K,E)𝐾𝐸(K,E) is simple, the claim follows.

The proof that C​(K,E)𝐶𝐾𝐸C(K,E) has Property (w) proceeds analogously. Let T𝑇T be an operator from C​(K,E)𝐶𝐾𝐸C(K,E) into M​(K,E′)𝑀𝐾superscript𝐸′M(K,E^{\prime}) represented by the measure G𝐺G, define Tx∈L​(C​(K),M​(K,E′′))subscript𝑇𝑥𝐿𝐶𝐾𝑀𝐾superscript𝐸′′T_{x}\in L(C(K),M(K,E^{\prime\prime})) as above by Tx​f=T​(f⊗x)subscript𝑇𝑥𝑓𝑇tensor-product𝑓𝑥T_{x}f=T(f\otimes x) for all x∈E,f∈C​(K)formulae-sequence𝑥𝐸𝑓𝐶𝐾x\in E,f\in C(K). If M​(K,E′)𝑀𝐾superscript𝐸′M(K,E^{\prime}) contains a copy of c0subscript𝑐0c_{0}, then ℓ1superscriptℓ1\ell^{1} embeds complementably in E𝐸E [8], a contradiction. Hence Txsubscript𝑇𝑥T_{x} does not fix a copy of c0subscript𝑐0c_{0}, and hence is weakly compact [7]. Thus G𝐺G is L​(E,M​(K,E′))𝐿𝐸𝑀𝐾superscript𝐸′L(E,M(K,E^{\prime}))-valued [5]. For all A∈Σ𝐴ΣA\in\Sigma, let

S=G​(A)′|C​(K,E):C​(K,E)→E′.:𝑆evaluated-at𝐺superscript𝐴′𝐶𝐾𝐸→𝐶𝐾𝐸superscript𝐸′S=G(A)^{\prime}|_{C(K,E)}:C(K,E)\to E^{\prime}.

O​B​S𝑂𝐵𝑆OBS is weakly compact by the claim above. It is easy to see that S′​x=G​(A)​xsuperscript𝑆′𝑥𝐺𝐴𝑥S^{\prime}x=G(A)x for all x∈E𝑥𝐸x\in E. Thus G​(A)=S′|E𝐺𝐴evaluated-atsuperscript𝑆′𝐸G(A)=S^{\prime}|_{E} is weakly compact. Finally, if ‖G‖norm𝐺\|G\| is not continuous at ∅\emptyset, we obtain, as in the proof of the claim, a pairwise disjoint sequence (An)subscript𝐴𝑛(A_{n}) in ΣΣ\Sigma, a ΣΣ\Sigma-measurable UEsubscript𝑈𝐸U_{E}-valued sequence of functions (fn)subscript𝑓𝑛(f_{n}) on K𝐾K, a sequence (gn)⊆UC​(K,E)subscript𝑔𝑛subscript𝑈𝐶𝐾𝐸(g_{n})\subseteq U_{C(K,E)}, and ϵ>0italic-ϵ0\epsilon>0 such that suppfn⊆Ansubscript𝑓𝑛subscript𝐴𝑛f_{n}\subseteq A_{n} and ∫fn​𝑑Ggn>ϵsubscript𝑓𝑛differential-dsubscript𝐺subscript𝑔𝑛italic-ϵ\int\!f_{n}dG_{g_{n}}>\epsilon for all n𝑛n. Now

S:C​(K,E)→ℓ1,S​g=(∫fn​𝑑Gg)n:𝑆formulae-sequence→𝐶𝐾𝐸superscriptℓ1𝑆𝑔subscriptsubscript𝑓𝑛differential-dsubscript𝐺𝑔𝑛S:C(K,E)\to\ell^{1},\hskip 10.00002ptSg=\biggl{(}\int\!f_{n}dG_{g}\biggr{)}_{n}

is bounded. Since (S​gn)​(n)>ϵ𝑆subscript𝑔𝑛𝑛italic-ϵ(Sg_{n})(n)>\epsilon for all n𝑛n, we obtain as before that ℓ1superscriptℓ1\ell^{1} embeds complementably into C​(K,E)𝐶𝐾𝐸C(K,E). By [8], this implies that ℓ1superscriptℓ1\ell^{1} embeds complementably into E𝐸E, a contradiction. Since the pair (K,E)𝐾𝐸(K,E) is simple, we conclude that T𝑇T is weakly compact. □□\Box

The pair (K,E)𝐾𝐸(K,E) is simple in either one of the following situations:

(a) E′superscript𝐸′E^{\prime} and E′′superscript𝐸′′E^{\prime\prime} both have the Radon-Nikodým Property [2];
(b) K𝐾K is a scattered compact [3].

Corollary 11

If the pair (K,E)𝐾𝐸(K,E) satisfies one of the conditions (a) or (b) listed above, then E𝐸E has Property (w) if and only if C​(K,E)𝐶𝐾𝐸C(K,E) does.

References

  • [1] Steven F. Bellenot, Richard Haydon, and Edward Odell, Quasi-reflexive and tree spaces constructed in the spirit of R. C. James, Contemporary Math. 85 (1989), 19-43.
  • [2] J. Brooks and P. Lewis, Linear operators and vector measures, Trans. Amer. Math. Soc. 192 (1974), 139-162.
  • [3] P. Cembranos, On Banach spaces of vector valued continuous functions, Bull. Austral. Math. Soc., 28 (1983), 175-186.
  • [4] J. Diestel and J. J. Uhl, Jr., “Vector Measures”, Math. Surveys, no. 15, Amer. Math. Soc., Providence, 1977.
  • [5] I. Dobrakov, On representation of linear operators in C0​(T,X)subscript𝐶0𝑇𝑋C_{0}(T,X), Czechoslovak. Math. J., 21 (1971), 13-30.
  • [6] Joram Lindenstrauss and Lior Tzafriri, “Classical Banach Spaces I, Sequence Spaces”, Springer-Verlag, Berlin, 1977.
  • [7] A. Pelczynski, On Banach spaces on which every unconditionally converging operator is weakly compact, Bull. Acad. Polon. Sci., 10 (1962), 641-648.
  • [8] E. Saab and P. Saab, A stability property of a class of Banach spaces not containing a complemented copy of ℓ1superscriptℓ1\ell^{1}, Proc. Amer. Math. Soc., 84 (1982), 44-46.
  • [9] E. Saab and P. Saab, Extensions of some classes of operators and applications, preprint.
